source_jobs.title: Forward Deployed Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
⛰️ About Northslope

The generational companies of the next century will run on mission-specific AI software that compounds their competitive advantage, not commoditized SaaS. We purpose-build production AI applications that enable our customers to operate at the speed, scale, and margins of an enterprise software company, in any industry.

We're building something fundamentally different: software that's as adaptable as the businesses it serves, created by engineers who understand both code and customer.



🛠️ The Role

As a Forward Deployed Software Engineer at Northslope, you will be responsible for helping our customers solve their most valuable problems, working closely with stakeholders to conceive, architect, build, and maintain applications that make a measurable impact on their business. Day to day, you’ll understand a customer’s most critical objectives and build operationalized workflows on their enterprise data that end users actually use to make change within the business. You’ll iterate quickly, build from scratch, and help organizations transform their operations at uncommon speed. As a Forward Deployed Engineer, you won’t just ship code - you’ll help reshape how entire organizations work by putting powerful tools in the hands of executives and real operators.



🎯 What We're Looking For

 - An obsession with helping your customer win, even when it goes beyond the original “scope”. You eat hard customer problems for breakfast and take pride in delivering solutions that actually drive value.

 - Strong analytical abilities and problem-solving aptitude. You love breaking down complex problems and building end-to-end solutions that actually work.

 - Proficiency in Python and/or Typescript. We build in these languages everyday.

 - Ability to work independently and own outcomes autonomously. You'll always have support, but we're looking for teammates who want to be in the driver's seat and fully own their work.

 - Demonstrated ability to build applications that use large structured and unstructured datasets in a production environment. We take a customer's data and make it valuable to them - this is our bread and butter.

 - A track record of delivering real-world outcomes with code. As a software engineer, data scientist, or deeply technical analytics lead, you've helped an organization up its game through the software you've written and/or the analytical products you've built.

 - Strong ability to collaborate with internal and external teammates with varying levels of technical proficiency. We embed with our customers and join their teams, you'll need both technical and people skills to succeed.

 - Willingness to travel as-needed for client implementation work. Every project is different, but typically projects involve travel 10-33% of the time.

 - Enthusiasm for working hybrid-in-person at our London or New York hub offices and onsite with our customers.  



🔍 Preferred but not required to be successful

 - A bachelor's or masters degree in Computer Science

 - Experience with Palantir Foundry and/or AIP

 - Experience delivering production ML and/or LLM-based solutions

 - Experience delivering production frontend applications in React

 - Experience with Spark and distributed compute.



💛 What We Offer

 - Competitive base salary + equity in the form of stock options

 - Comprehensive benefits package including health insurance (inclusive of dental and vision) and 401k matching

 - Flexible hybrid work environment

 - The opportunity to build solutions, systems, and software from the ground up as we scale

 - A small, tight-knit team where your contributions directly impacts our ability to execute on our mission

 - Occasional travel (less than 10% of your time) for company offsites where you'll connect with teams across our New York and London hubs



🛸 Our Principles

 - Only Valuable Problems: Not every problem is worth solving. We work on the projects that will significantly improve our customers' bo

source_jobs.title: Principal, Business Development
source_jobs.description:
⛰️ About Northslope

The generational companies of the next century will run on mission-specific AI software that compounds their competitive advantage, not commoditized SaaS. We purpose-build production AI applications that enable our customers to operate at the speed, scale, and margins of an enterprise software company, in any industry.

We're building something fundamentally different: software that's as adaptable as the businesses it serves, created by engineers who understand both code and customer.



🛠️ The Role

This is not your typical enterprise sales role. It is an opportunity for proven leaders excited about owning and building a business within a high growth start-up. At Northslope, we create bespoke AI applications for our customers that deliver lasting solutions to previously unsolvable problems. There are no playbooks. We seek the rare individual who possesses a deep quantitative and strategic background, who is driven to solve complex business problems, has a track record of delivering complex solutions for ambitious clients, and understands that revenue is core to any business. If your passion is for understanding the why behind a problem and building a business from the ground up, this is the role for you.



✍️ What you’ll do

 - Lead and execute the entire deal lifecycle for multi-million dollar deals, focusing on creative deal structuring and mutual value creation, not just a transactional close.

 - Forge unique, long-term partnerships that deliver outsized value over many years, going deeper than rinse and repeat contracts.

 - Act as a cross-functional leader, collaborating with product, engineering, and legal teams to shape go-to-market strategies and land creative deals.

 - Apply your analytical and strategic mindset to deconstruct complex, nebulous business problems and architect novel solutions that leverage AI software to drive measurable impact.

 - Bridge the gap between business strategy and technical execution, engaging in high-level architectural discussions and demonstrating technical credibility to a diverse range of stakeholders.
   
   

🎯 What We're Looking For

 - Proven Track Record of Delivery: A demonstrable history of building and delivering on partnerships that solve complex business problems for customers.

 - A Builder's Mindset: You are not a cog in a machine; you are an entrepreneur who identifies high-level business problems and applies a strategic mindset to create innovative, value-driving solutions.

 - Quantitative Foundation: A foundational education in a quantitative field from a top US university (e.g., Science, Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics, Economics). This rigorous background is the starting point for your approach to problem-solving and value creation.

 - Executive Presence: Exceptional communication, presentation, and negotiation skills with a history of engaging with and influencing C-suite and executive-level audiences.

 - Technical Affinity: An interest in or understanding of software architecture and development principles, with the ability to discuss complex technical concepts in an accessible manner.

 - Adaptability: Ability to thrive in ambiguity and a strong bias toward action in unscripted environments.



🛸 Our Principles

 - Only Valuable Problems: Not every problem is worth solving. We work on the projects that will significantly improve our customers' bottom lines.

 - Outcomes, Not Activity: We create value, not extract it. We focus on our business impact, not racking up billable hours.

 - Forward Deployed Engineering: We never build in a vacuum. We go to the heart of the problem and build alongside our users.

 - One size fits none. Generic software fails the most important customers. We build for the specific — the exact industry, workflow, data, and competitive context of the company we're serving.



🚠 Why Northslope

At Northslope, we're built different. We take pride in being more like a product startup than a traditional services f

source_jobs.title: Deployment Strategist (UK Defence)
source_jobs.description:
⛰️ About Northslope

The generational companies of the next century will run on mission-specific AI software that compounds their competitive advantage, not commoditized SaaS. We purpose-build production AI applications that enable our customers to operate at the speed, scale, and margins of an enterprise software company, in any industry.

We're building something fundamentally different: software that's as adaptable as the businesses it serves, created by engineers who understand both code and customer.



🛠️ The Role

At Northslope Technologies, our Deployment Strategists play a critical role in solving complex business challenges through strategic data-driven solutions. Day to day, you’ll understand a customer’s most critical objectives and build operationalized workflows on their enterprise data that end users actually use to make change within the business. You’ll iterate quickly, build from scratch, and help organizations transform their operations at uncommon speed.

At Northslope Technologies, our Deployment Strategists are transformative problem-solvers who bridge the gap between complex data and strategic business outcomes. In this role, you'll work directly with enterprise clients to develop innovative solutions that drive meaningful operational change. You will write code and ship production software. You can do it all.



🎯 What We're Looking For

 - Customer-Centric Problem Solving: A relentless commitment to customer success that extends beyond contractual obligations. You eat hard customer problems for breakfast and take pride in delivering transformative solutions.

 - Analytical Excellence: Exceptional ability to deconstruct intricate problems and develop comprehensive, high-impact solutions that deliver tangible business value.

 - Autonomous Delivery: Strong capacity for independent work with a proactive approach to project ownership. You'll drive outcomes while leveraging team support.

 - Data Intuition: Proven expertise in managing and deriving insights from both structured and unstructured large-scale datasets in production environments. You aren't afraid to get your hands dirty writing code.

 - Collaborative Versatility: Outstanding interpersonal skills enabling effective communication and collaboration across technical and non-technical stakeholders.

 - Willingness to travel as-needed for client implementation work. Every project is different, but typically projects involve travel 10-33% of the time.

 - Candidates with current UK security clearance, previously held clearance, or eligibility to obtain a clearance are strongly preferred
   
   

🔍 Preferred but not required to be successful

 - Experience delivering production enterprise AI solutions

 - Experience with Palantir Foundry and/or AIP

 - Familiarity with or proficiency in Typescript or Python



💛 What We Offer

 - Competitive base salary + equity in the form of stock options

 - Comprehensive benefits package including health insurance (inclusive of dental, vision, mental health), pension matching, and commuting via Cycle2Work benefits.

 - Flexible hybrid work environment

 - The opportunity to build solutions, systems, and software from the ground up as we scale

 - A small, tight-knit team where your contributions directly impacts our ability to execute on our mission

 - Occasional travel (less than 10% of your time) for company off-sites where you'll connect with teams across our New York and London hubs



🛸 Our Principles

 - Only Valuable Problems: Not every problem is worth solving. We work on the projects that will significantly improve our customers' bottom lines.

 - Outcomes, Not Activity: We create value, not extract it. We focus on our business impact, not racking up billable hours.

 - Forward Deployed Engineering: We never build in a vacuum. We go to the heart of the problem and build alongside our users.

 - One size fits none. Generic software fails the most important customers. We build for the specific — the exact

source_jobs.title: Forward Deployed Software Engineer, New Grad
source_jobs.description:
⛰️ About Northslope

The generational companies of the next century will run on mission-specific AI software that compounds their competitive advantage, not commoditized SaaS. We purpose-build production AI applications that enable our customers to operate at the speed, scale, and margins of an enterprise software company, in any industry.

We're building something fundamentally different: software that's as adaptable as the businesses it serves, created by engineers who understand both code and customer.



🛠️ The Role

At Northslope, we believe in giving early-career engineers the opportunity to make immediate, tangible impact. As a Forward Deployed Software Engineer at Northslope, you will be responsible for helping our customers solve their most valuable problems, working closely with users to conceive, architect, build, and maintain applications that make a measurable impact on their business. Day to day, you’ll understand a customer’s most critical objectives and build operationalized workflows on their enterprise data that end users actually use to make change within the business. You’ll iterate quickly, build from scratch, and help organizations transform their operations at uncommon speed.



This is not a typical new grad role. You won’t be stuck maintaining legacy code or working on backend services no one sees. Instead, you’ll partner closely with customer users, understand their real-world problems, and ship end-to-end applications that improve business outcomes. Think of it as business strategy meets engineering — fast-paced, high-impact, and deeply collaborative.



🎯 What We Look For

 - An obsession with helping your customer win, even when it goes beyond the original “scope”. You eat hard customer problems for breakfast and take pride in delivering solutions that actually drive value.

 - Strong analytical abilities and problem-solving aptitude. You love breaking down complex problems and building end-to-end solutions that actually work.

 - Proficiency in coding fundamentals.

 - Ability to work independently and own outcomes autonomously. You'll always have support, but we're looking for teammates who want to be in the driver's seat and fully own their work.

 - Strong ability to collaborate with internal and external teammates with varying levels of technical proficiency. We embed with our customers and join their teams, you'll need both technical and people skills to succeed.

 - Willingness to travel as-needed for client implementation work. Every project is different, but typically projects involve travel 10-33% of the time.

 - Enthusiasm for working hybrid-in-person at our London or New York hub offices and onsite with our customers.

 - Engineering degree, preferred in Computer Science or related field. 2025 or 2026 Graduates.



💛 What We Offer

 - Competitive base salary + equity in the form of stock options

 - Comprehensive benefits package including health insurance (inclusive of dental and vision) and 401k matching

 - Flexible hybrid work environment

 - The opportunity to build solutions, systems, and software from the ground up as we scale

 - A small, tight-knit team where your contributions directly impacts our ability to execute on our mission

 - Occasional travel (less than 10% of your time) for company off-sites where you'll connect with teams across our New York and London hubs



🛸 Our Principles

 - Only Valuable Problems: Not every problem is worth solving. We work on the projects that will significantly improve our customers' bottom lines.

 - Outcomes, Not Activity: We create value, not extract it. We focus on our business impact, not racking up billable hours.

 - Forward Deployed Engineering: We never build in a vacuum. We go to the heart of the problem and build alongside our users.

 - One size fits none. Generic software fails the most important customers. We build for the specific — the exact industry, workflow, data, and competitive context of the company we're serving.

source_jobs.title: Business Operations and Strategy
source_jobs.description:
⛰️ About Northslope

The generational companies of the next century will run on mission-specific AI software that compounds their competitive advantage, not commoditized SaaS. We purpose-build production AI applications that enable our customers to operate at the speed, scale, and margins of an enterprise software company, in any industry.

We're building something fundamentally different: software that's as adaptable as the businesses it serves, created by engineers who understand both code and customer.



🛠️ The Role

We are looking for an ambitious and thoughtful operator to join our team at the intersection of account expansion, business operations, and commercial strategy. This role is about unlocking value: identifying where we’re leaving money on the table and building systems to ensure we never do. You’ll work closely with our implementation teams, project leads, and executive stakeholders to drive customer expansions, enable strategic renewal conversations, and support pre-sales efforts through compelling value storytelling. In this role, you will help us institutionalize smart habits. This is an ideal opportunity for someone with a consulting background who is passionate about technology and AI.



🎯 What We're Looking For

 - Experience in management consulting or business operations/strategy role, ideally at a top-tier or boutique firm, with additional start-up experience as a plus

 - Strong executive presence, with the ability to communicate confidently and concisely with senior stakeholders

 - A knack for visual storytelling — you know how to build decks that land with business leaders and drive decisions via PowerPoint/Google Slides

 - Comfort and fluency with numbers— whether it’s building simple models or helping quantify impact, you're confident working in Excel/Google Sheets

 - Comfortable working in ambiguity and wearing multiple hats across pre-sales, account strategy, and internal ops

 - A proactive and resourceful approach — you don’t wait to be told what to do; you anticipate needs, connect dots, and make things happen

 - Curiosity and interest in the world of AI, data platforms, and transforming how organizations operate — no prior experience required, but motivation matters

 - Motivated by outcomes — you take ownership, move fast, and care deeply about delivering value

 - Hunger to grow — you want to stretch beyond your current skill set and contribute to a company that’s scaling fast



💛 What We Offer

 - Competitive base salary + equity in the form of stock options

 - Comprehensive benefits package including health insurance (inclusive of dental and vision) and 401k matching

 - Flexible hybrid work environment

 - The opportunity to build solutions, systems, and software from the ground up as we scale

 - A small, tight-knit team where your contributions directly impacts our ability to execute on our mission

 - Occasional travel (less than 10% of your time) for company offsites where you'll connect with teams across our New York and London hubs



🛸 Our Principles

 - Only Valuable Problems: Not every problem is worth solving. We work on the projects that will significantly improve our customers' bottom lines.

 - Outcomes, Not Activity: We create value, not extract it. We focus on our business impact, not racking up billable hours.

 - Forward Deployed Engineering: We never build in a vacuum. We go to the heart of the problem and build alongside our users.

 - One size fits none. Generic software fails the most important customers. We build for the specific — the exact industry, workflow, data, and competitive context of the company we're serving.



🚠 Why Northslope

At Northslope, we're built different. We take pride in being more like a product startup than a traditional services firm. We value velocity, ingenuity, and grit, and we relentlessly focus on delivering tangible outcomes for our customers.

We also have fun. We get to work on big, hard problems, in a fast-paced environmen

source_jobs.title: Full Stack Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
⛰️ About Northslope

The generational companies of the next century will run on mission-specific AI software that compounds their competitive advantage, not commoditized SaaS. We purpose-build production AI applications that enable our customers to operate at the speed, scale, and margins of an enterprise software company, in any industry.

We're building something fundamentally different: software that's as adaptable as the businesses it serves, created by engineers who understand both code and customer.



🛠️ The Role

As a Full-Stack Software Engineer at Northslope, you’ll be responsible for architecting, building, and scaling high-impact applications across the entire stack—front-end, back-end, and everything in between. Working closely with internal teams and stakeholders, you’ll deliver products that turn complex datasets into mission-critical workflows, tools, and insights.

You’ll spend your time designing user-centric UIs, building robust APIs, and operationalizing data pipelines. You’ll own projects end-to-end, ship fast, and play a pivotal role in transforming the operations of high-impact teams tackling complex real-world problems.

At Northslope, we believe great software engineers are also great product thinkers. You won’t just write clean code—you’ll help design the right solutions and see them through to production, adoption, and impact.



🎯 What We're Looking For

 - User Obsession – You’re laser-focused on making your users successful, even when it means flexing outside your lane. You take pride in building solutions that truly drive business value.

 - Full-stack engineering mindset – You’re comfortable jumping between the front-end and back-end as needed. You have experience building modern software / AI applications and RESTful APIs, and can stitch it all together to deliver seamless end-user experiences.

 - Frontend Development Skills – You're proficient in TypeScript and React, with a track record of building modern, responsive web applications. You understand component architecture, hooks, state management, and performance optimization. You care about creating intuitive, accessible user interfaces that delight users and work seamlessly across devices.

 - Ownership Mentality – You thrive when given autonomy and responsibility. You’re eager to take the lead on problems, with support from the team when needed.

 - Track Record of Building in Production – You’ve built and maintained software systems that operate in the real world, ideally with complex or large-scale data. You care deeply about performance, reliability, and user feedback.

 - Data Fluency – You're experienced working with structured and unstructured datasets, and know how to translate data into action via your code.

 - Collaboration Skills – You can communicate and partner with teammates across disciplines and customers with varying technical backgrounds.



🔍 Preferred but not required to be successful

 - A bachelor's or master’s degree in Computer Science

 - Experience delivering production-grade applications

 - Experience with backend frameworks (FastAPI, Node.js http://node.js)

 - Experience with modern CSS frameworks like Tailwind CSS

 - Familiarity with frontend build tools and bundlers (Vite, Webpack)

 - Knowledge of frontend testing frameworks and best practices

 - Familiarity with distributed compute systems like Spark

 - Experience with Palantir Foundry and/or AIP

 - Exposure to ML or LLM solutions in production environments



💛 What We Offer

 - Competitive base salary + equity in the form of stock options

 - Comprehensive benefits package including health insurance (inclusive of dental and vision) and 401k matching

 - Flexible hybrid work environment

 - The opportunity to build solutions, systems, and software from the ground up as we scale

 - A small, tight-knit team where your contributions directly impacts our ability to execute on our mission

 - Occasional travel (less than 10% of your time) for company off

source_jobs.title: Forward Deployed Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
⛰️ About Northslope

The generational companies of the next century will run on mission-specific AI software that compounds their competitive advantage, not commoditized SaaS. We purpose-build production AI applications that enable our customers to operate at the speed, scale, and margins of an enterprise software company, in any industry.

We're building something fundamentally different: software that's as adaptable as the businesses it serves, created by engineers who understand both code and customer.



🛠️ The Role

As a Forward Deployed Software Engineer at Northslope, you will be responsible for helping our customers solve their most valuable problems, working closely with stakeholders to conceive, architect, build, and maintain applications that make a measurable impact on their business. Day to day, you’ll understand a customer’s most critical objectives and build operationalized workflows on their enterprise data that end users actually use to make change within the business. You’ll iterate quickly, build from scratch, and help organizations transform their operations at uncommon speed. As a Forward Deployed Engineer, you won’t just ship code - you’ll help reshape how entire organizations work by putting powerful tools in the hands of executives and real operators.



🎯 What We're Looking For

 - An obsession with helping your customer win, even when it goes beyond the original “scope”. You eat hard customer problems for breakfast and take pride in delivering solutions that actually drive value.

 - Strong analytical abilities and problem-solving aptitude. You love breaking down complex problems and building end-to-end solutions that actually work.

 - Proficiency in Python and/or Typescript. We build in these languages everyday.

 - Ability to work independently and own outcomes autonomously. You'll always have support, but we're looking for teammates who want to be in the driver's seat and fully own their work.

 - Demonstrated ability to build applications that use large structured and unstructured datasets in a production environment. We take a customer's data and make it valuable to them - this is our bread and butter.

 - A track record of delivering real-world outcomes with code. As a software engineer, data scientist, or deeply technical analytics lead, you've helped an organization up its game through the software you've written and/or the analytical products you've built.

 - Strong ability to collaborate with internal and external teammates with varying levels of technical proficiency. We embed with our customers and join their teams, you'll need both technical and people skills to succeed.

 - Willingness to travel as-needed for client implementation work. Every project is different, but typically projects involve travel 10-33% of the time.

 - Enthusiasm for working hybrid-in-person at our London or New York hub offices and onsite with our customers.
   
   

🔍 Preferred but not required to be successful

 - A bachelor's or masters degree in Computer Science

 - Experience with Palantir Foundry and/or AIP

 - Experience delivering production ML and/or LLM-based solutions

 - Experience delivering production frontend applications in React

 - Experience with Spark and distributed compute.



💛 What We Offer

 - Competitive base salary + equity in the form of stock options

 - Comprehensive benefits package including health insurance (inclusive of dental, vision, mental health), pension matching, and commuting via Cycle2Work benefits.

 - Flexible hybrid work environment

 - The opportunity to build solutions, systems, and software from the ground up as we scale

 - A small, tight-knit team where your contributions directly impacts our ability to execute on our mission

 - Occasional travel (less than 10% of your time) for company off-sites where you'll connect with teams across our New York and London hubs



🛸 Our Principles

 - Only Valuable Problems: Not every problem is worth solving. We work on the p
